figure_panel: B. Jobs per USD 1 million investment/spending

figure_panel: B. Jobs per USD 1 million investment/spending
series_label: Fossil fuels
source_title: Green versus brown: Comparing the employment impacts of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and fossil fuels using an input-output model
projected_vs_realised: projected (I-O model; based on historical economic structure)
original_verified_value: 7.49 (renewables), 7.72 (energy efficiency), 2.65 (fossil fuels) FTE jobs per $1 million spending
metric_definition: Total employment multiplier (direct + indirect + induced) from US input-output model: full-time equivalent jobs supported per $1 million of final demand/spending in each sector. Derived from 2006-2010 IMPLAN data and sector-specific vectors.
notes: ACCEPT (core comparator). High-quality, widely cited study on green vs brown job creation per dollar invested. Explicit on page 2. Key insight: EE and RE generate ~3x more jobs per $ than fossil. Labor intensity, domestic content, and compensation drive differences. Minor caveat: US-centric, pre-2016 data but methodology robust and transparent. Duplicate extraction exists (0009); use this as primary. Excellent for investment-to-jobs elasticity in energy/infra policy.
